title: Stream Mining / Hoeffding Trees
summary: A progressive implementation of data stream mining algorithms, starting from the foundational VFDT (Hoeffding Tree) for learning from stationary high-speed streams, extending to CVFDT for handling concept drift in time-changing streams, adding ADWIN for adaptive windowing drift detection, culminating in the MOA framework with ensemble methods, and SAM-KNN for self-adjusting memory-based classification under heterogeneous concept drift.
instructionPreview: Implement a data stream mining framework in Python. The workspace contains base components: stream generators (RandomTreeGenerator, RotatingHyperplaneGenerator, WaveformGenerator), evaluation utilities (prequential evaluation), base classifier classes, and attribute observers.
instruction:
Implement a data stream mining framework in Python. The workspace contains base
components: stream generators (RandomTreeGenerator, RotatingHyperplaneGenerator,
WaveformGenerator), evaluation utilities (prequential evaluation), base classifier
classes, and attribute observers.

Implement five modules in order:
1. VFDT (Hoeffding Tree) - incremental decision tree using Hoeffding bound
2. CVFDT - extends VFDT with sliding window and concept drift handling
3. ADWIN - adaptive windowing drift detector with exponential histogram
4. MOA framework - adds OzaBagging ensemble and evaluation task runner
5. SAM-KNN - self-adjusting memory kNN with dual STM/LTM stores

Each module has a requirements YAML in /workspace/requirements/ describing the
algorithm details and experiments to run. The run.sh script must output experiment
results in the format: EXPERIMENT_NAME: VALUE (one per line).

Key function signatures:
- HoeffdingTree.train(instance), .predict(instance), .get_tree_size()
- CVFDT.train(instance), .predict(instance) with window management
- ADWIN.add_element(value), .detected_change(), .get_estimation()
- OzaBagging.train(instance), .predict(instance) with Poisson(1) resampling
- EvaluateInterleavedTestThenTrain.run() returning accuracy metrics
- SAMModel.partial_fit(features, label), .predict(features)

moduleDag.description: A progressive implementation of data stream mining algorithms, starting from the foundational VFDT (Hoeffding Tree) for learning from stationary high-speed streams, extending to CVFDT for handling concept drift in time-changing streams, adding ADWIN for adaptive windowing drift detection, culminating in the MOA framework with ensemble methods, and SAM-KNN for self-adjusting memory-based classification under heterogeneous concept drift.

nodes.label: Mining High-Speed Data Streams (VFDT / Hoeffding Tree)
nodes.description: Implements the Very Fast Decision Tree learner that builds decision trees incrementally from data streams using the Hoeffding bound for split decisions. Uses constant memory and time per example.

nodes.label: Mining Time-Changing Data Streams (CVFDT)
nodes.description: Extends VFDT with a sliding window, alternate subtree growing, and split validity checking to handle concept drift in time-changing data streams.

nodes.label: ADWIN: Adaptive Windowing for Concept Drift Detection
nodes.description: Implements the ADWIN algorithm using exponential histogram compression to maintain a variable-length window. Detects distribution changes via the Hoeffding bound and shrinks the window to discard stale data.

nodes.label: MOA: Massive Online Analysis
nodes.description: Provides the MOA evaluation framework including OzaBagging ensemble method with Poisson(1) resampling, EvaluateInterleavedTestThenTrain task runner, and performance statistics collection.

nodes.label: SAM-KNN: Self-Adjusting Memory with kNN
nodes.description: Implements SAM-KNN with dual memory (Short-Term and Long-Term), kNN-based prediction, adaptive memory selection based on local accuracy evaluation, and LTM cleaning/compression for concept drift adaptation.

edges.label: CVFDT extends VFDT to handle concept drift
ADWIN provides drift detection complementing VFDT split decisions
MOA provides comprehensive framework implementing VFDT/CVFDT and ensembles
SAM-KNN uses concept drift awareness from CVFDT for memory management
SAM-KNN can leverage ADWIN for detecting when to clean LTM
